question: Did the model correctly select Option C or explicitly state that the two shapes are 'Not the same'?
Did the model correctly identify the objects in the image as exactly two puzzle pieces?
Did the model correctly identify the colors of the pieces, noting that the left piece is black (or dark gray) and the right piece is red?
Did the model correctly observe that the left (black) piece has an indentation (hole/socket) on its right side?
Did the model correctly observe that the right (red) piece has a protrusion (knob/tab) on its left side?
Did the model avoid hallucinating that the two puzzle pieces have the exact same shape geometry (e.g., claiming they are identical or just rotated versions of each other)?
Did the model provide a logical reasoning that links specific structural differences (such as the indentation vs. the protrusion) to the conclusion that the shapes are not the same?
Did the model provide a clear selection from the provided options (A, B, or C) rather than an open-ended refusal?
